name: Lernaean Hydra
description: The Lernaean Hydra was a serpentine water monster with multiple heads, said to be the offspring of Typhon and Echidna. It dwelled in the swamps of Lerna near Argos. Each time one head was severed, two more would grow back in its place, making it an exceptionally difficult creature to kill. Its breath and blood were poisonous, and the Hydra was feared throughout the land. The Hydra was a formidable opponent, and its defeat was considered one of Heracles' most challenging labors. The creature's immortality, through the regrowth of its heads, made it a symbol of resilience and the difficulty of overcoming persistent evils. The hydra's venom was also used to poison Heracles' arrows, adding another layer of danger and legacy to the monster. It represented a primal, untamed force of nature, a threat to both the land and the people who lived near it. Heracles eventually overcame the hydra with the help of Iolaus, who cauterized the neck stumps after each head was severed to prevent regrowth.
subtitle: Serpentine monster with regenerative heads
abilities: Regeneration (two heads grow back when one is severed)
appearance: The Lernaean Hydra is depicted as a massive serpentine creature with multiple heads, ranging from five to nine, and in some accounts even more. Its scales are dark and tough, providing it with natural armor. Its breath is poisonous, and its blood is highly venomous. The heads are reptilian, with sharp teeth and piercing eyes. The Hydra's overall appearance is fearsome and intimidating, reflecting its dangerous nature.
habitat: Swamps and marshes, particularly the swamps of Lerna near Argos.
behavior: The Lernaean Hydra is an aggressive and territorial creature, fiercely protective of its swampy domain. It ambushes prey from the murky waters, using its multiple heads to attack from different angles. It is highly venomous and uses its poisonous breath to weaken or kill its victims. The Hydra is also known for its regenerative abilities, making it a difficult opponent to defeat.

keyMyths.title: The Second Labor of Heracles
keyMyths.description: Heracles was tasked with killing the Lernaean Hydra as his second labor. He eventually defeated it with the help of Iolaus, who cauterized the neck stumps to prevent the heads from regrowing.

sources.author: Pseudo-Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive source on Greek mythology, detailing the myth of the Lernaean Hydra.
sources.text: A comprehensive source on Greek mythology, detailing the myth of the Lernaean Hydra.

sources.author: Euripides
sources.description: A play that references the Hydra and Heracles' labor.
